name: Jerez del Marquesado y Cogollos de Guadix — rodeo por el Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada
region_intro: Hiking around Jerez Del Marquesado offers access to the high mountain environments of the Sierra Nevada Natural Park. The region is characterized by dramatic canyons, glacial formations, and extensive forests, with many trails ascending above 2,000 meters. Hikers can explore river systems like the Alcázar River and encounter diverse flora along the routes. This mountainous terrain provides a challenging setting for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Jerez Del Marquesado The most popular hiking route is Refugio Postero Alto – Picón de Jerez loop from Peñón del Mojo, a 12.7 miles (20.5 km) trail that takes 10 hours 46 minutes to complete. This challenging route offers expansive views of the surrounding peaks. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Tizná Recreation Area – Alcázar Ravine loop from Jérez del Marquesado, a difficult 8.4 miles (13.6 km) path. This trail explores the scenic Alcázar Ravine and the popular La Tizná recreational area. Local hikers also love the Refugio Postero Alto – Paratas de Chorreras Negras loop from Refugio Picón de Jérez, a 6.9 miles (11.2 km) trail leading through high mountain landscapes, often completed in about 5 hours 2 minutes. Hiking around Jerez Del Marquesado is defined by high mountain peaks, deep ravines, and forested paths, with options primarily for experienced hikers seeking challenging ascents. The routes in Jerez Del Marquesado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Jerez Del Marquesado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Jerez Del Marquesado?
A: There are 15 hiking trails documented around Jerez Del Marquesado. The majority of these, 12 routes, are classified as difficult, with 3 routes considered moderate. This region is primarily suited for experienced hikers seeking challenging ascents in a high mountain environment.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking near Jerez Del Marquesado?
A: Hiking around Jerez Del Marquesado offers a diverse range of dramatic landscapes within the Sierra Nevada Natural Park. You'll encounter high mountain environments, impressive glacial formations, deep canyons like the Alcázar Ravine, and extensive forests, including ancient chestnut groves. Many trails ascend above 2,000 meters, providing breathtaking vistas of surrounding peaks and high-altitude lakes.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking trails in Jerez Del Marquesado?
A: The hiking trails around Jerez Del Marquesado are predominantly challenging, with no routes specifically classified as easy. However, some of the moderate trails, particularly those starting from areas like La Tizná Recreation Area, might be suitable for families with older children or those accustomed to longer walks. Always check the route details and elevation gain before setting out with family.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Jerez Del Marquesado?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Jerez Del Marquesado are designed as circular routes, allowing you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31574359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Postero Alto – Picón de Jerez loop from Peñón del Mojo&lt;/a&gt; or the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14598836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tizná Recreation Area – Alcázar Ravine loop from Jérez del Marquesado&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or points of interest along the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore dramatic canyons such as Canyon Alhori and Alcázar Ravine, and discover beautiful high-altitude lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hondera Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;. The demanding climb to Laguna Hondera also features waterfalls formed by the Chorreras Negras. Additionally, you'll find impressive peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7096934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picón de Jérez 3,088 m&lt;/a&gt; and the historic Acequia del Corazón, a water channel built at about 2200 meters altitude.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trailheads?
A: Key starting points for many trails often have designated parking areas. For instance, the Refugio Postero Alto, located at 1,880 meters, serves as a significant starting point for numerous mountain excursions. Another popular access point is La Tizná Recreation Area, which is about 10 kilometers from the village and accessible via a forest track, likely offering parking facilities.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Jerez Del Marquesado?
A: The best time for hiking in Jerez Del Marquesado is generally during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when temperatures are milder and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower altitudes, while winter offers challenging conditions suitable for experienced hikers, with snow often covering the higher peaks.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to mountain huts or refuges?
A: Yes, several trails in the area lead to or pass by mountain huts. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6960243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Postero Alto&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent mountain hut at 1,880 meters and serves as a starting point for many routes, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31574678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paratas de Chorreras Negras – Refugio Postero Alto loop from Jérez del Marquesado&lt;/a&gt;. Other refuges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/112263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cucaracha Refuge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4246025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Campiñuela Bivouac Shelter&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Jerez Del Marquesado?
A: The hiking experiences in Jerez Del Marquesado are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the challenging nature of the routes, the stunning high-mountain vistas, the dramatic canyons, and the sense of immersion in the Sierra Nevada's wild landscapes. The diverse terrain, from forested paths to rocky ascents, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to see while hiking?
A: Yes, the region has a rich history that can be explored on foot. You might encounter remnants of medieval fortifications, Mudejar churches in the village, and the Torreón de Jérez del Marquesado, an Andalusian-period tower next to Canyon Alhori. Some trails also pass by abandoned farmhouses and former mining operations, such as the Santa Constanza Mine, offering a glimpse into the area's past.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of high-altitude lakes?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra Nevada region around Jerez Del Marquesado is home to several beautiful high-altitude lakes. The demanding climb to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hondera Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering stunning views of this natural feature. The Seven Lagoons are also considered among the most beautiful places in the Sierra Nevada National Park, accessible via more challenging routes.
tips: Alrután is a gravel track without steep slopes that offers incredible views. You will climb through a forest of different types of trees typical of the area.

The town of Cogollos de Guadix is located between the Lugros river and the Bernal ravine, within the Sierra Nevada natural park. The origins of the town date back to the Late Roman Empire. In the mountains, around the municipality, you can still see the ruins of the ancient Roman settlement.

Jerez del Marquesado is another of the beautiful towns in the Guadix region. With a rich historical heritage, it invites you to rest from pedaling in its streets, and leaves you wanting to return to explore its tourist attractions in depth.

waypoints.name: Parish of Santa María, Cogollos de Guadix
